condition_pratyaya.description: If set, rule only applies when the active pratyaya matches

attrs.translation: The vowels ā, ai, au are called vṛddhi
The vowels a, e, o are called guṇa
An ik vowel is replaced by the corresponding yaṇ semivowel when followed by a vowel
e and o are replaced by ay and av before a vowel
When a/ā is followed by a simple vowel, guṇa replaces both
When a/ā is followed by e, o, ai, or au, vṛddhi replaces both
A simple vowel followed by its savarṇa (homogeneous vowel) is replaced by its long form
The sUP suffixes are ordained after nominal stems
After a-stem, śas is replaced by n in masculine
Before am pratyaya, the prior vowel prevails (absorbs the a of am)
The su of sambodhana is deleted (luk) after a short vowel
After a-stem, bhis is replaced by ais
After a-stem, ṭā→ina, ṅasi→t, ṅas→sya
After a-stem, ṅe is replaced by ya
nuṬ augment is prefixed to am (gen pl) after short vowel
Final a of aṅga is lengthened before yaÑ-initial suffix
Extends 7.3.101 to sUP context
Final a of aṅga becomes e before jhaL-initial suffix in bahuvacana
Final a of aṅga becomes e before os (gen/loc dual)
Word-final s (and ṣ of sajuṣ) is replaced by ru (= r, u is it-marker)
r (from ru-substitution) becomes visarjanīya at avasāna (pause/word-end) or before khar consonant
s becomes ṣ after i/u/ṛ/e/o when in an ādeśa or pratyaya

a → e before os (gen/loc dual) (Aṣṭ. 7.3.104)
s → ru (= r) word-finally (sasajuṣo ruḥ, Aṣṭ. 8.2.66)
r → ḥ at avasāna (kharavasānayorvisarjanīyaḥ, Aṣṭ. 8.3.15)
s → ṣ after i/u/ṛ/e/o in pratyaya (Aṣṭ. 8.3.59)
ac = all vowels {a, i, u, ṛ, ḷ, e, o, ai, au}
ik = simple vowels excluding a {i, u, ṛ, ḷ}
yaṇ = semivowels {y, v, r, l}
aṇ = the three short simple vowels {a, i, u}
eṅ = guṇa diphthongs {e, o}
a is a short simple vowel and is itself a guṇa vowel
e is a guṇa-grade diphthong vowel (guṇa of i/ī)
o is a guṇa-grade diphthong vowel (guṇa of u/ū)
ai is a vṛddhi-grade diphthong vowel (vṛddhi of i/ī)
au is a vṛddhi-grade diphthong vowel (vṛddhi of u/ū)
